headlines.title: Seattle May Day 2026 rallies in Cal Anderson Park for downtown march
headlines.summary: May Day organizers call for 'No Work, No School, No Shopping' with messages focusing on communities over ICE, workers over billionaires, and services over war.

headlines.title: Seattle Mayor Katie Wilson calls for Starbucks boycott
headlines.summary: Mayor Wilson's first move after winning election was calling for a Starbucks boycott, prompting the company to consider relocating to Nashville.

headlines.title: Seattle weighs pause on new data center proposals
headlines.summary: City officials consider pausing new data center proposals as multiple plans emerge, though details remain early and unclear.

headlines.title: Paid parking permits start at Northgate and Shoreline Sound Transit garages
headlines.summary: Starting May 1, commuters will need paid permits for parking at busy light rail stations, ending the early morning rush for spots.

headlines.title: Seattle Public Schools implements new cellphone policy
headlines.summary: New 'Off And Away for the Day' rules for grades K-8 and 'No Cell Bell to Bell' for high schoolers takes effect district-wide.

top_story.title: Seattle May Day 2026: Thousands expected at Cal Anderson Park rally
top_story.summary: May Day organizers are planning a national day of action with a rally at Cal Anderson Park followed by a march downtown, calling for 'No Work, No School, No Shopping' to send messages about communities over ICE, workers over billionaires, and services over war.
